rationale: The only direct evidence is a single DOI match (10.1509/jmr.10.0510) with no citation context or quoted reference text, so we cannot tell how the 2012 RTF paper is used in the 2015 lay rationalism article. Given both papers involve consumer-behavior individual-difference scale development/validation, a non-incidental use (e.g., benchmark/discriminant or methodological precedent) is plausible, so it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only.
